subject: Revert back to staging for Portal
commit_message:
Revert back to staging for Portal

Change-Id: Iab437f98f5f3c23491ff41eed8a01f143370bc75
Issue-ID: PORTAL-580
Signed-off-by: Yang Xu <yang.xu3@huawei.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab controller: Deployment put on queue, number 1 on the queue.

Patch Set 1:

Gitlab Orange 2: Chained-CI build started, max time: 10800 seconds

Patch Set 1:

Build Started http://onapci.org/jenkins/job/oom-verify/331/

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Build Failed 

http://onapci.org/jenkins/job/oom-verify/331/ : FAILURE

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/2352/

Patch Set 1:

We have to temporarily rollback to staging images when waiting for the new release, so that our CI can work

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/2352/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/2352

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Why not just override them using the staging docker manifest?

Patch Set 1:

> Why not just override them using the staging docker manifest?

There are other teams outside integration don't use override file, and they are actively testing

Patch Set 1:

As far as I can tell this is a timing/load issue on portal db startup, and changing it back to 2.5.0-STAGING-latest will not fix the problem; there's also been no change in portal repos since 2.5.0 was released, so 2.5.0-STAGING-latest should have identical content as 2.5.0.

Portal team will need to release 2.5.1 if they want to try a fix.  In the mean time, teams that want to test with the staging version should override the portal versions with the next staging version 2.5.1-STAGING-latest.

Patch Set 1:

> As far as I can tell this is a timing/load issue on portal db
 > startup, and changing it back to 2.5.0-STAGING-latest will not fix
 > the problem; there's also been no change in portal repos since
 > 2.5.0 was released, so 2.5.0-STAGING-latest should have identical
 > content as 2.5.0.
 > 
 > Portal team will need to release 2.5.1 if they want to try a fix. 
 > In the mean time, teams that want to test with the staging version
 > should override the portal versions with the next staging version
 > 2.5.1-STAGING-latest.

I had a meeting with Portal team. We looked at the release version and don't find SHA256 matching with any of staging image. Besides, Intel team also saw the issue today in their lab.

Patch Set 1:

> > As far as I can tell this is a timing/load issue on portal db
 > > startup, and changing it back to 2.5.0-STAGING-latest will not
 > fix
 > > the problem; there's also been no change in portal repos since
 > > 2.5.0 was released, so 2.5.0-STAGING-latest should have identical
 > > content as 2.5.0.
 > >
 > > Portal team will need to release 2.5.1 if they want to try a fix.
 > > In the mean time, teams that want to test with the staging
 > version
 > > should override the portal versions with the next staging version
 > > 2.5.1-STAGING-latest.
 > 
 > I had a meeting with Portal team. We looked at the release version
 > and don't find SHA256 matching with any of staging image. Besides,
 > Intel team also saw the issue today in their lab.

I updated Portal on OOM-Daily and SB-01 with Portal STAGING-latest today, and both fixed the issue.

Patch Set 1:

I agree Portal should update their image version to 2.5.1. Do you want to help them as you did for SO?

Patch Set 1:

We need the portal team to version bump since they keep their pom.xml version in sync with the docker version; it's not just changing a single file like for SO.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Ok, something is really wrong with portal 2.5.0 images.  My pull says they were created 13 months ago.  I think we need LF to re-release 2.5.0 for portal.

Patch Set 1:

Looks like LF released portal-app:2.5.0 using portal-app:latest, which was created 13 months ago.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

At this point I'd prefer that we don't roll back, and just wait for LF to re-release the correct portal 2.5.0 images.

Patch Set 1:

Yes, please do not revert back to staging images. If LF team can release the right docker images, then the issue will be fixed.

Patch Set 1:

> Yes, please do not revert back to staging images. If LF team can
 > release the right docker images, then the issue will be fixed.

Ok. the time is my concern. Please push LF to release asap. Many teams are testing outside integration.

Abandoned

New Portal images were released
